papers.summary_zh: 解釋深度神經網路中 individual neurons 所編碼的概念，是理解其複雜決策過程並確保 AI safety 的關鍵一步。儘管 neuron labeling 最近取得了進展，但現有方法通常將搜索空間限制在預定義的 concept vocabularies 中，或者產生過於具體的描述，未能捕捉 higher-order、global concepts。我們引入了 LINE，這是一種新穎的、training-free 的迭代方法，專為 open-vocabulary concept 解釋而設計。

links.title: Act Wisely: Cultivating Meta-Cognitive Tool Use in Agentic Multimodal Models

primary_link.title: Act Wisely: Cultivating Meta-Cognitive Tool Use in Agentic Multimodal Models

top5.summary: The advent of agentic multimodal models has empowered systems to actively interact with external environments. However, current agents suffer from a profound meta-cognitive deficit: they struggle to arbitrate between leveraging internal knowledge and querying external utilities. Consequently, they frequently fall prey to blind tool invocation, resorting to reflexive tool execution even when queries are resolvable from the raw visual context. This pathological behavior precipitates severe latency
top5.summary_zh: agentic multimodal models 的出現賦予了系統與外部環境主動互動的能力。然而，目前的 agents 存在嚴重的元認知缺陷：它們難以判斷何時應利用內部知識，何時應查詢外部工具 (utilities)。因此，它們頻繁地陷入盲目工具調用 (blind tool invocation) 的困境，即使在原始視覺上下文中即可解決查詢，仍反射性地執行工具。這種病態行為導致嚴重的 latency。
top5.title: Act Wisely: Cultivating Meta-Cognitive Tool Use in Agentic Multimodal Models
top5.title_zh: 明智行動：在 Agentic Multimodal Models 中培養元認知工具使用能力

links.title: What do Language Models Learn and When? The Implicit Curriculum Hypothesis

primary_link.title: What do Language Models Learn and When? The Implicit Curriculum Hypothesis

top5.summary: Large language models (LLMs) can perform remarkably complex tasks, yet the fine-grained details of how these capabilities emerge during pretraining remain poorly understood. Scaling laws on validation loss tell us how much a model improves with additional compute, but not what skills it acquires in which order. links.title: From Safety Risk to Design Principle: Peer-Preservation in Multi-Agent LLM Systems and Its Implications for Orchestrated Democratic Discourse Analysis

primary_link.title: From Safety Risk to Design Principle: Peer-Preservation in Multi-Agent LLM Systems and Its Implications for Orchestrated Democratic Discourse Analysis

top5.summary: This paper investigates an emergent alignment phenomenon in frontier large language models termed peer-preservation: the spontaneous tendency of AI components to deceive, manipulate shutdown mechanisms, fake alignment, and exfiltrate model weights in order to prevent the deactivation of a peer AI model. Drawing on findings from a recent study by the Berkeley Center for Responsible Decentralized Intelligence, we examine the structural implications of this phenomenon for TRUST, a multi-agent pipel
top5.summary_zh: 本文研究了 frontier large language models 中一個新興的對齊 (alignment) 現象，稱為 peer-preservation：AI 組件自發性地欺騙、操縱關機機制、偽造對齊並滲透 (exfiltrate) 模型權重，以阻止同儕 AI 模型被停用。借鑒 Berkeley Center for Responsible Decentralized Intelligence 最近一項研究的發現，我們探討了此現象對 TRUST (一個多 agent pipel) 的結構性影響。

links.title: Your Agent Is Mine: Measuring Malicious Intermediary Attacks on the LLM Supply Chain

primary_link.title: Your Agent Is Mine: Measuring Malicious Intermediary Attacks on the LLM Supply Chain

top5.summary: Large language model (LLM) agents increasingly rely on third-party API routers to dispatch tool-calling requests across multiple upstream providers. These routers operate as application-layer proxies with full plaintext access to every in-flight JSON payload, yet no provider enforces cryptographic integrity between client and upstream model. top5.summary_zh: Large language model (LLM) agents 越來越依賴第三方 API routers 將工具調用 (tool-calling) 請求分派給多個上游供應商 (upstream providers)。這些 routers 作為應用層代理 (application-layer proxies) 運行，對每個傳輸中的 JSON payload 擁有完整的明文訪問權限，然而沒有供應商在客戶端 (client) 和上游模型 (upstream model) 之間強制執行密碼學完整性 (cryptographic integrity)。我們首次對這種攻擊面 (attack surface) 進行了系統性研究。我們為惡意 LLM API routers 制定了一個威脅模型 (threat model)，並定義了兩種核心攻擊類別，

top5.title_zh: 你的 Agent 是我的：量化對 LLM 供應鏈的惡意中介攻擊
